name: Inanna
shortDescription: Inanna is the ancient Sumerian goddess of love, beauty, sex, war, justice and political power. She is a central figure in Mesopotamian mythology.
description: Inanna, the Queen of Heaven, stands as one of the most prominent and complex deities in the Sumerian pantheon. Her origins are rooted in ancient Mesopotamia, where she was worshipped as the goddess of love, beauty, sex, procreation, and also, surprisingly, war and justice. She was not merely a goddess of fertility; she embodied the dynamic forces of life and death, creation and destruction. Inanna's power extended into the political sphere, as she was considered the patron deity of the city of Uruk, and her favor was essential for legitimate kingship. Her most famous myth is her descent into the underworld, a journey fraught with peril and sacrifice, ultimately leading to her death and resurrection, symbolizing cyclical renewal. This myth highlights her complex nature and her willingness to confront even the darkest aspects of existence. She is associated with the planet Venus and is often depicted with symbols such as the eight-pointed star, lions, and bundles of reeds. Her worship involved elaborate rituals, hymns, and prayers, reflecting her profound influence on Sumerian society and culture. Inanna's legacy continued in later Mesopotamian cultures, where she was known as Ishtar, and her influence can be seen in other goddesses of love and war in various mythologies.

sources.title: Inanna, Queen of Heaven and Earth
sources.author: Diane Wolkstein and Samuel Noah Kramer

sources.title: Mythology: Timeless Tales of Gods and Heroes
sources.author: Edith Hamilton
